issues.description: Implement CLI with query mode (load, query, print, exit) and server mode (load once, serve HTTP queries). Python via Click+uvicorn, Rust via clap+axum. POST JSON dict-in/dict-out protocol. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 7: CLI (Query + Server)

issues.description: Given a YAML file defining tables, GSIs, and associations, when I call Database.from_schema(path, data_dir), it should create a fully configured database. Declarative, versionable, shared between Rust and Python. Spec: features/schema/schema_from_yaml.feature
issues.title: Schema from YAML

issues.description: Given generated fixture data, when I run benchmarks (load-time, query-time, cache, concurrency, memory), each scenario should produce valid timing output. Visualization generates SVG charts and a markdown report. Spec: features/benchmarks/benchmark_scenarios.feature
issues.title: Benchmark scenarios and visualization

issues.description: Given a database under concurrent read load, when N threads query simultaneously, all should return consistent results with no partial reads or crashes. Spec: features/concurrency/concurrent_reads.feature
issues.title: Concurrent reads

issues.description: Given a table with registered on_put and on_delete hooks, when records are put or deleted, the hooks should fire after the operation. Hook errors are logged but do not block the operation. Spec: features/core/table_event_hooks.feature
issues.title: Table event hooks

issues.description: Given filesystem edge cases (file appears/disappears during scan, truncated JSON), Virtuus should handle all gracefully without crashing. Corrupted files are reported as errors, valid records remain accessible. Spec: features/concurrency/file_race_conditions.feature
issues.title: File race conditions

issues.description: Primary deliverable. Pluggable fixture generation (social_media, ecommerce, complex_hierarchy profiles), load-time/query-time/cache/concurrency/memory benchmarks, SVG chart visualization, regression detection. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 10: Benchmarks & Visualization

issues.description: Given a database with multiple tables, when I call db.describe(), it should return a complete schema overview: all tables with their GSIs, associations, record counts, and staleness status. Spec: features/query/database_describe.feature
issues.title: Database describe

issues.description: Implement hash-partitioned index with optional sorted range key. GSIs support partition-only and partition+sort configurations, with put/remove/update operations and query with sort conditions and direction. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 2: GSI (Global Secondary Index)

issues.description: Given a database with GSI-indexed tables, when I execute() with index and sort directives, it should use the GSI for efficient lookup with optional sort conditions and direction. Spec: features/query/execute_indexed.feature
issues.title: Execute indexed queries

issues.description: Given a has_many_through association with a junction table, when resolved, it should query the junction table's GSI then PK-lookup each target record. Spec: features/associations/has_many_through.feature
issues.title: Has-many-through associations

issues.description: Given a sort condition with any DynamoDB-style operator (eq, ne, lt, lte, gt, gte, between, begins_with, contains), when evaluated against a value, it should return the correct boolean result. Handles edge cases: null values, numeric vs string comparison, empty strings. Spec: features/sort/sort_conditions.feature
issues.title: Sort condition operators

issues.description: Given concurrent deletes and association resolution, the system should return either a valid result or clean null/empty, never a dangling reference or crash. Spec: features/concurrency/referential_integrity_under_load.feature
issues.title: Referential integrity under load

issues.description: Implement standalone predicate evaluators matching DynamoDB KeyConditionExpression operators: eq, ne, lt, lte, gt, gte, between, begins_with, contains. No dependencies on other modules. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 1: Sort Conditions

issues.description: Implement mtime-based cache layer with two-tier staleness detection (O(1) dir check, O(N) file scan), JIT invalidation on query, warm reindex for proactive refresh, and incremental reload of only changed files. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 4: Cache & Incremental Invalidation

issues.description: Given a table with GSIs attached, when records are put or deleted, GSIs should be automatically maintained. Query via GSI returns full records from the table. Spec: features/core/table_gsi_integration.feature
issues.title: Table GSI integration

issues.description: Given a database with belongs_to associations, when I call db.validate(), it should walk all FKs and verify each resolves to a record in the target table. Returns a list of violations. Spec: features/query/database_validate.feature
issues.title: Database validate (referential integrity)

issues.description: Given a database with tables, when I call warm(), all tables should proactively refresh without waiting for a query. When already fresh, warm is a no-op. Spec: features/cache/warm_reindex.feature
issues.title: Warm reindex

issues.description: Given a table, when I create a GSI with a partition key (and optionally a sort key), it should be registered and ready to index records. Supports hash-only and hash+range configurations. Spec: features/gsi/gsi_creation.feature
issues.title: GSI creation

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Phase 0 scaffold complete. All deliverables implemented:
- VERSION file (0.1.0)
- Python package: pyproject.toml, behave.ini, .coveragerc, src/virtuus/__init__.py, src/virtuus/_python/__init__.py
- Shared BDD: features/environment.py, features/steps/smoke_steps.py (7 steps covering all 3 smoke scenarios)
- Rust crate: Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs (version() + #[cfg(test)] unit tests), src/bin/virtuus.rs, tests/cucumber.rs
- tools/check_spec_parity.py (checks asymmetry, not completeness)
- Makefile with check, coverage-python, coverage-rust, check-parity, build targets
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- AGENTS.md updated with code quality standards (Black+Ruff+D rules, Sphinx docstrings, 100% coverage)
- Ruff D rules enabled; module docstring added to virtuus/__init__.py

make check result: Python 100% coverage, Rust 100% coverage, parity 7/7 steps, all lint clean.

issues.description: Implement the Phase 0 project skeleton: VERSION file, Python package (pyproject.toml, behave, coverage), Rust crate (Cargo.toml, lib.rs, CLI bin, cucumber integration tests), shared step definitions in features/steps/, tools/check_spec_parity.py, Makefile, and CI workflow. All make coverage-python, make coverage-rust, and make check-parity must pass at 100%.
issues.title: Phase 0 scaffolding implementation

issues.description: Given a data directory and schema, when I run virtuus serve, it should load data once and serve queries over HTTP. POST /query for dict-in/dict-out queries. Additional endpoints: GET /health (server status), POST /describe (schema metadata), POST /validate (referential integrity check), POST /warm (proactive refresh). Spec: features/cli/server_mode.feature
issues.title: CLI server mode

issues.description: Given a populated GSI, when I query by partition key (with optional sort conditions and direction), it should return matching record PKs in the correct order. Empty partitions return empty results. Spec: features/gsi/gsi_query.feature
issues.title: GSI query

issues.description: Given a database with tables, when I call execute() with where, pk, fields, or limit directives, it should return matching records with correct field projection and limits. Non-existent table raises an error (fail fast). Non-existent GSI name raises an error. Spec: features/query/execute_basic.feature
issues.title: Execute basic queries

issues.description: Given a table with associations pointing to itself (parent/child trees), when resolved, it should correctly traverse the hierarchy without infinite loops. Spec: features/associations/self_referential.feature
issues.title: Self-referential associations

issues.description: Given the virtuus library is available in both Python and Rust, it should load and report a valid version string. Both implementations read from a shared VERSION file at the repo root, ensuring version parity. This validates the project skeleton, shared Gherkin runner, coverage tooling, and CI pipeline all work end-to-end. Spec: features/smoke/smoke_test.feature
issues.title: Library availability smoke test

issues.description: Given a table name and primary key field, when I create a table, it should be ready to store and retrieve records by PK. Supports simple PK and composite PK (partition + sort key). Spec: features/core/table_creation.feature
issues.title: Table creation

issues.description: Given a database with associations between tables, when I execute() with include directives, it should recursively resolve associations and return nested results for all association types and multi-level depth. Spec: features/query/execute_nested.feature
issues.title: Execute nested queries with includes

issues.description: Given a table with in-memory records, when I call export(directory), it should write all records to JSON files using atomic writes. The inverse of load. Spec: features/core/table_export.feature
issues.title: Table export

issues.description: Given a GSI, when records are put/removed/updated, the index should maintain correct bucket membership. Updates remove the old entry and insert the new one atomically. Spec: features/gsi/gsi_operations.feature
issues.title: GSI operations

issues.description: Given a table with a has_many association defined, when I resolve the association for a record, it should query the foreign table's GSI to find all related records. Spec: features/associations/has_many.feature
issues.title: Has-many associations

issues.description: Compile Rust library as Python extension module via PyO3+maturin. All existing Gherkin specs must pass with both pure-Python and Rust backends. Transparent fallback import pattern. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 9: PyO3 Bridge

issues.description: Given a fixture profile (social_media, ecommerce, complex_hierarchy) and scale factor, when I generate fixtures, it should produce the correct number of records with valid associations and realistic data distribution. Spec: features/benchmarks/fixture_generation.feature
issues.title: Benchmark fixture generation

issues.description: Implement Table with PK-based storage, GSI maintenance, file-backed persistence via atomic writes, and quality-of-life features: count, describe, validation, export, event hooks. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 3: Table (Core CRUD + File Persistence + QoL)

issues.description: Given a table loaded from a directory, when files on disk change (modified, added, deleted), the cache should detect staleness via two-tier mtime checking. JIT refresh happens automatically before query results are returned. Configurable: check_interval prevents re-checking within the interval; auto_refresh=False disables JIT refresh, requiring explicit warm() calls. Spec: features/cache/cache_invalidation.feature
issues.title: Cache invalidation

issues.description: Given readers and writers operating simultaneously, readers should never see partially-written records and writers should never corrupt the index for concurrent readers. Spec: features/concurrency/concurrent_read_write.feature
issues.title: Concurrent read-write

issues.description: Given files changing on disk while queries are in flight, readers should get either the old state or the new state, never a mix. JIT refresh + concurrent reads must be safe. Spec: features/concurrency/concurrent_refresh.feature
issues.title: Concurrent refresh

issues.description: Given a table with a belongs_to association defined, when I resolve the association for a record, it should look up the parent record by PK in the foreign table. Spec: features/associations/belongs_to.feature
issues.title: Belongs-to associations

issues.description: Given a table, when I put/get/delete/upsert records, the table should maintain correct state. Supports scan, all, and bulk load operations. Also supports composite primary keys (partition + sort key): put/get/delete with composite PK, and two records with same partition but different sort keys coexist. Spec: features/core/table_crud.feature
issues.title: Table CRUD

issues.description: Given a data directory and query parameters, when I run virtuus query, it should load data, execute the query, print JSON results, and exit. Spec: features/cli/query_mode.feature
issues.title: CLI query mode

issues.description: Implement Database container with nested dict query interface (execute), cursor-based pagination, field projection, database-level describe/validate diagnostics, and declarative YAML schema loading. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 6: Database + execute() + Schema-from-YAML

issues.description: Given a database under concurrent write load, when N threads call put() simultaneously, every record should be persisted with no lost writes, corrupted files, or duplicate PKs. Concurrent puts to the same PK use last-write-wins: no error, final state is one of the written values. Spec: features/concurrency/concurrent_writes.feature
issues.title: Concurrent writes

issues.description: Given a table backed by a directory, when I put a record it should be written to a JSON file on disk. Filename convention: {pk}.json for simple PKs, {partition}__{sort}.json for composite PKs. PKs containing path separators (/ or \) are rejected. When I delete, the file should be removed. Load reads all JSON files from the directory. All writes use atomic temp+rename. Non-JSON files are ignored. Spec: features/core/table_file_persistence.feature
issues.title: Table file persistence

issues.description: Implement has_many (GSI query on foreign table), belongs_to (PK lookup), has_many_through (junction table), and self-referential associations (parent/child trees). See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 5: Associations

issues.description: Given the Rust library compiled as a Python extension via PyO3+maturin, all existing Gherkin specs should pass with both pure-Python and Rust backends. The import fallback is transparent to consumer code. No new feature files; uses existing specs.
issues.title: PyO3 bridge with transparent fallback

issues.description: Set up the full project skeleton with dual Rust+Python structure, shared Gherkin specs, and coverage tooling. One smoke-test feature passes in both languages at 100% coverage from the first commit. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 0: Project Scaffolding + Coverage Infrastructure

issues.description: Given a table with records, when I call count() or count(index, value), it should return the count without materializing full result lists. Spec: features/core/table_count.feature
issues.title: Table count

issues.description: Given a stale table, when refreshed, only added/modified/deleted files should be reloaded - not the entire table. GSIs must be updated for all changed records. Spec: features/cache/incremental_refresh.feature
issues.title: Incremental refresh

issues.description: Given a table with opt-in validation enabled, when I put a record missing the PK field or GSI-indexed fields, it should warn or error based on configuration. Default is silent. Spec: features/core/table_validation.feature
issues.title: Table validation

issues.description: Given a table, when I call check(), it should return what WOULD change (added/modified/deleted files) without actually refreshing. The on_refresh hook fires after actual refreshes with a change summary. Spec: features/cache/cache_check.feature
issues.title: Cache check (dry-run refresh)

issues.description: Given a table, when I call describe(), it should return a metadata dict with name, PK, GSI definitions, association names, record count, and staleness status. REPL-friendly output. Spec: features/core/table_describe.feature
issues.title: Table describe

issues.description: Stress-test under concurrent operations: parallel reads, parallel writes, mixed read-write, refresh during queries, filesystem race conditions, and referential integrity under load. Forces architectural decisions about locking. See Vision.md for full details.
issues.title: Phase 8: Concurrency & Integration Testing

issues.description: Given a large result set, when I execute() with limit and next_token directives, it should return paginated results with cursor-based pagination matching the DynamoDB pattern. Supports descending sort direction: pagination with sort_direction desc traverses in correct reverse order. Full traversal yields all records with no duplicates. Spec: features/query/execute_pagination.feature
issues.title: Execute paginated queries
